review_text: Quite expensive for a powered site, even though it is somewhat out of Byron Bay. Grounds are untidy, required mowing and sites for annex are horrible sandy small gravel. Should have at least green matting down. Amenities are old and run down. The entry door at amenities had water pooled at the front step for our entire stay, a safety hazard. Basin area has been upgraded, however drains are blocking up. Showers are old and run down with one not even working, definately require an upgrade.Not worth the money. Would not recommend.
Always have the best time here. Cabins are lovely and clean, staff were amazing, helpful and friendly. Love the new cafe aswell! Cafe staff smiling and happy.

We stay at their ensuite site for 2 night. People camping there are very friendly. But the Laundry was old and one of the dryer took the money (by credit card type) yet not working. One gentle man before me and one young lady after me all encountered this problem. We told the office yet they said nothing and take no action to put sign to avoid the issues. How many victims will be….

Lovely park. Stayed in a pet friendly cabin which was much nicer than the regular 'box type' prefabricated cabins one finds in most caravan parks.  Very nice walk to beautiful Tallow Beach. Friendly staff.  Would stay again.

Our family villa was the perfect size for us. Was comfortable, just a bit of wear and tear as the villa seemed old, cracks in the roof, rattling of the house when it rained or was windy. Door accessing the main room had no blinds so was hard to sleep with light shining in.. staff were exceptionally amazing. The area is great and lots of fun things to do. I will definitely stay again.
Great location, welcoming staff, all amenities are in great working order. What more could you want? We will be back!
Very clean and great service and location
Beautiful place

Due to wildfires we requested to change the date on our booking to earlier as we could no longer stay at the places we were staying before. The lady stated “it is not amendable as you received heavy discount”… it was $6 off. We said we don’t mind paying extra to change it but she refused. Very unhelpful with a lack of empathy. Poor service.

Skye had the best time.  She could run off lead on the nearby beach. Her mums took her to the agility park onsite, where she played with other visiting dogs.  In summary, this holiday park offers great pet friendly accommodation options.  We were less than a 5min drive from town, main beach and the twilight market.  Would definitely stay here again.

Camping trop cher… (payé 65$/nuit, pour un emplacement sans power)

Emplacements très petits.

Les toilettes et douches sont mixtes, ce qui peut mettre mal à l’aise.
De plus, il y en a beaucoup trop peu pour tout le camping, et les cabines douches et toilettes sont dans la même pièce (comme des mini salles de bains).
Ce qui fait que les sanitaires sont extrêmement à sales…

Dommage
Such a cute and clean cabin — totally recommend it!

Would not stay here again…. EVER! ||We were a family staying on a powered site ($180 per night/minimum 4 night stay) with friends staying on adjacent site and both in camper/trailers facing each other.  There was about 8 feet of space between canopies. Reliance on the good will of neighbouring sites is important as the sites are small and offer absolutely no privacy. There is only room for one vehicle per site and that has to be parked side on. No big deal but if there are massive vehicles either side of yours then getting out takes more than a 3 point turn. ||Bathrooms are horrendous. The worst I have experienced anywhere while camping all around Australia or overseas. There were just 7 showers (one was out of service) available for females (8 for males) IN TOTAL, for a completely full park that has over 150 sites. Work out the ratio of people per shower even taking into account ensuited caravans and the several stand alone ensuites.  Not good, nor very hygienic. Matted hair clogged the drains. |Don’t get me started on the toilets! Six stand alone toilets IN TOTAL. There’s another 8 in the male/female shower cubicles but they can only be accessed when shower is not in use.  I’ve never had to queue for 30 minutes to use a toilet before. But I did at this park. |I could only find one shower/toilet available for those with a disability. |Hiring portable amenities blocks at peak times would solve all these issues and make for happier campers.  ||There were several positives. The staff were friendly and helpful and the walk to the beach was interesting. The beach itself was amazing. |The pool is small but clean.|Fire pits were also allowed but finding space was a challenge.|The park is situated close to a golf club and to Suffolk Park which probably has the best bakery and Italian restaurant in NSW. The local SPAR supermarket also has everything one needs when on a camping holiday. |The park is situated in a nice area but unfortunately appears to be very run down and owners reluctant to make any improvements. ||It’s so much easier and more pleasant to give a glowing review of places I’ve stayed but sadly on this occasion it’s not to be.

We stayed in one of the glamping tents for four nights and we really enjoyed it, the tents were great and well cleaned, really cute set up, the amenities were clean and supplied shampoo which was a nice touch, the pool was nice and it wasn’t too far out of town and there was a free shuttle bus, we would definitely come back again.
蠻漂亮的帳篷房間，離海灘走路十分鐘，晚上星空非常漂亮，離市區開車只要五分鐘，價格非常有性價比，非常推薦

The lady at reception was really kind and polite, she gave us an upgrade to a Ecovillage Pod and it was AMAZING!!Very clean, modern, welcoming: we didn't want to leave!The breakfast we had was delicious as well, fresh croissants and high quality cappuccino to start the day in the best way.Very much recommended, we can't wait to be back❤️
Great location next to the beach and close to town.

Probably the busiest site I've been so far. But it is well maintained. Lots of people can be a bit overwhelming but I always felt good about where I'm. Good amenities, lovely pool, friendly staff. Most of the spots are flat. Getting in town could be challenging on a Sunday. All in all loved the stay

We stayed in the Hideaway Tent for 4 nights & absolutely loved it!!! We’ve been away travelling for nearly 4 months & this was our favourite stop of the trip. The room itself was so comfortable and felt very luxurious. Clean amenities around the site & plenty of kitchen space so we never had to wait to cook. It was nice that you could pay contactless for laundry, as we’ve been to lots of coin-operated laundrettes and never have the right change! Friendly staff and a lovely coffee shop open in the morning too :) despite being located a bit further from the Main Beach area, we managed fine with the bus. They aren’t very frequent throughout the day but is a quick & easy way of getting around if you time it right!

Really enjoyed a couple of nights here. Very quite and relaxed atmosphere with great facilities. Plenty of shade and birds which we loved to see (& hear). The staff were all very friendly. There was a problem with our cabin and we were upgraded to a clamping tent without any fuss or needing to ask. Recovery from a problem sorts the amateurs from professionals and the team smashed it. Others need to learn from that! Will definitely aim to come back.
No complaints only long walk if driving a truck

Great place, I got away for a self-holiday of solitude & it’s perfect for this, they have a pool, onsite Brekky, onsite massage, beach is close, beach shack is so cute & has air con & parking(as I have lambo so was essential) best of all the staff are super friendly & kind. Highly recommend!!!
Simple and basic

We loved our two night stay in the Luxury Tent. Just beautiful with lovely park outlook from generous deck area with string lights, bbq and bath. Nicely decorated and well equipped. Very clean and bed and pillows were comfy. Staff were lovely and the gift of wine for our honeymoon was a nice surprise. Short drive into Byron. We only wished we could have stayed longer!

It's a must place to stay . from  booking in to departure just a fantastic experience . Pet friendly and there is a fantastic paved walkway down to a off leash beach and a fantastic dog wash aswell . amenities were very clean and smelt nice . staff were friendly and helpful . Camp kitchen was spotless . Be aware when cooking in the camp kitchen , do not get tricked by the kookaburras ,one will land on the table and while you're taking photos of it , it's mate flies in and steals your meat of the BBQ . cute my arse .
Great stay ,would recommend to anyone . Regards steve

We had a lovely stay at Byron Bay Holiday Park. The staff were amazing—very helpful and friendly. The facilities were excellent, and our villa was spotless and fully equipped with everything we needed.

Me and my family stay here for 5days last
week, we had an amazing time. The staff was really friendly and we are so happy with our villa, everything is perfect.  We hoping to come back again soon. Thank you again

Terrible spot for camping, with your tent or campervan. The campsite is right beside a busy road. The spots for camping have very few trees that make it very hot.  The facility has some cabins that are in a nice, shaded area; but the options for camping are veyr unpleasant! I can't wait to leave this campsite.

In terms of the location, it is quite far from the city. The beach is a 10 minutes walk through a natural area that is marked for prevalent sighting of brown snakes.

The positives: 1) The staff do their best to address guests' concerns, but they did not offer anything to help me feel a bit better about this stay. 2) We had a powered site wiith en-suite. The bathroom was very clean and modern. 3) The facility offers the optino to rent bikes.
Shipping container hotels but clean and tidy and the newer ones were really lovely (in the video).
Super camping, l’un des meilleurs que j’ai fait de ma vie, piscine, plein d’équipement, plusieurs cuisines, plein d’emplacement pour recharger les téléphones je recommande grandement

Sites are absolutely tiny. I felt as though our neighbouring sites may have been part of the family - there's no possibility of privacy or personal space. Despite the park being half empty, we kept having people allocated right next to us, over and over again. Our site was predominantly dirt, scattered with cigarette butts and not a single speck of shade. Bathrooms were poorly ventilated and dirty, with bugs flying in and around all of the sanitary bins. Not within walking distance of the main stores or restaurants. The nearby road is quite busy and produces a lot of noise. Staff were helpful and friendly, but sadly I wouldn't consider this a trip I want to remember.

We booked a Superior Cabin at Ingenia Holidays Byron Bay based on the photos of the website. It was anything but superior. The cabin was in need of serious repairs and maintenance. |In the kitchen the laminate was chipped along the cupboards, the skirting board had fallen off and was just left leaning against where it should be attached. The front sliding door was dirty and mouldy. |In the bathroom the metal fixtures were rusty,  a bad grout repair had been attempted around the basin, tiles missing, cupboard door hinge broken. |We had to stand on the bed to close the Venetian blinds as the rods for turning the slats were missing. Overall an unsafe, unclean experience.

Loved our stay here! Our cabin was so nice, spacious, clean with all kitchen facilities. A small decking area. The park has a nice pool, but my favourite is being able to walk to the beach and catch sunrise each morning. The track to the beach is so beautiful. The park is big, options to camp. Caravan, kids play area. Will definitely be back for sure.

We have just spent 2 nights in our van at Ingenia Holidays Byron Bay.
We had an issue as we were leaving and the staff were absolutely wonderful in helping my husband with our van.
The park is right on the beach and close to a number of attractions.
Thank you for helping us this morning it was greatly appreciated!
Very nice motorhome stop. Great amenities onsite wwith a lovely swimming pool.

Nous avons séjourné la nuit du 8 au 9 février 2026 avec un camping car de 7m.
Au premier abord tout paraissait bien, même si les emplacements sont un peu trop serrés.
Des jeunes gens on commencé à être très bruyants au niveau de la cuisine pour le dîner, puis ils sont allés sur leur emplacement, pas très loin du notre puis ils ont chanté, crié, hurlé, tambouriné, fait de la musique avec certainement des casseroles et cela jusqu'à 01h50 du matin.
Est ce normal !
Personne n'est internevu.
Ça énerve !!
Nous sommes partis après très peu d'heures de sommeil, et vraiment très déçus.
Great, friendly staff. Every where clean. Great facilities

We were welcomed by the Lovely Lilly! We intended to just stay for 2 nights, the holiday site was so lovely, clean and welcoming we stayed a whole week!!! Tahlia who is the Barista at the coffee shop was an absolute gem! Thank Byron Bay!!!!

We booked for 5 nights and ended up extending our stay to 9. We had our van and stayed in an ensuite space. The parks location was only a few minutes to town. It allowed dogs. My wife takes her chihuahua everywhere so that was very important. The ensuite was clean and tidy with great water pressure. We were parked under a large tree and while we had some shad we also had Ibis roosting in the tree and pooping all over our van. Not the parks fault but something to be aware of.
Overall, staff were friendly and helpful. The park was clean and tidy. It does get busy even during the week with a lot of mobile campers coming and going but over all we were very happy with the park and will definitely be back. In fact we have booked for March.
Super camping. Tout a disposition.
Good place to stay. Had a great time with family. Well maintained cleanliness of the place. Good swim area with basketball court and playground 🛝 👍🏼

Nice and quiet. I stayed at powered spot with rooftop tent. Simple clean ammeneties with hot water showers. Nice rest areas and multiple open kitchen corners with fridges, stoves etc. Small pool to jump in.

Had an overnight stay, the cabins were very clean just enough room for two, would come stay there again

Thank u 😊
Avoid Cabin 83, we were woken at 4am by pine cone bombs dropping on the roof where we were sleeping.

We had a one-week booking at Ingenia Holidays and had a very disappointing experience. First night, while we were in the middle of having dinner, the park manager came over and told us we had to stop eating immediately and leave, even though we were not making any noise or disturbing anyone.

She said that because it was almost 10pm (there were literally 4 minutes left), we were not allowed to stay any longer and warned us that every extra minute would cost us a lot of money, which felt intimidating and unnecessary.

We ended up staying the night and leaving in the morning, but the way this situation was handled was rude, stressful and completely unprofessional, especially considering we had a full week reserved.

This is not how paying guests should be treated. Very disappointing and would not recommend.
The toilet is small and there is no room service if you are planning to stay for a longer perio

Site were really small and packed in for tents and campers. Roads in campground gravel and hard for kids to ride scooters. Bathrooms always seem to be closed for cleaning but also always felt unclean. ||Some of our group stayed in the safari tents and they were awesome with incredible private views.
Great place a bit outside of Byron Bay. Still close enough to get to the town quickly. Good kids facilities. Nice cabins if you don't travel with a caravan. All well and good!
Cabins aren’t super clean, floors were a bit dirty
Love this park, quiet and friendly. Great access walk to the beach. Staff are lovely. Great variety of cabins and glamping options as well as good size sites.

Had a great stay over the new years. The camp kitchen had everything you needed to cook up. More toilets & showers are needed for the campers, always had a waiting line for them. 10min walk to the beach, recommend take a bike or ebike to head into town
The park has cabins, tent area and is generally peaceful and relatively quiet a great place to relax and unwind. Beach access has been restored with the bridge getting a partial rebuild.

Most unpowered campsites are really small and some are located right next to the road.
There are not enough amenities in the unpowered camping area - I‘ve never had to wait that long for a shower on any other campground in Australia and the cleanliness wasn’t great either.
The location is okay, a short drive to Byron Bay and a ten minute walk to a lovely beach.

Really good paid for stay in Byron, Bus takes picks you up just outside and can take you in and out of Byron all day for next to nothing.|The campsite is clean and tidy, staff are friendly,  has a great little pop up coffee station on site.||But I really must say thank you and shoutout the staff on the whole, I managed to lose a rather expensive Tudor watch, taking it off in the shower and not remembering to put it back on. The cleaning staff handed it it and I managed to reclaim it. This just wouldn’t happen at most places I feel, and definitely wouldn’t happen in England where I am from.

We had two nights staying on an unpowered site. We have a fairly large caravan and struggled to fit. I noticed that all sites were very small and everyone was really jammed in like sardines. I’m glad that we only were staying there for two nights in transit to our next location. I would certainly be pretty disappointed if I was staying there for a longer holiday.

The bathrooms were appalling in our area. There were four women’s and four men’s bathrooms, including shower. When I took my kids to shower, I could not find a clean shower cubicle to use. The two available had hair clogging up the drains and was all over the floor. It must be a common problem because there’s a large squeegee mop which you’re encouraged to use after showering. There were two unisex toilets available and they stunk of urine. The flooring of these toilets were wooden floorboards and I think that urine had soaked into the floorboards overtime and there’s probably nothing that can be done to remove that smell. It was really disgusting.

Otherwise, the location is fine. There’s a small pool and a small playground which my kids enjoyed. There is a jumping pillow which was not properly inflated so could not be used. My kids were fairly disappointed. I scoping out other caravan parks in the area before booking here.

I will note, however, that the staff were great on check-in.

Great service and location, has a short walking trail to tallow beach which is a quiet and nice beach. The studio room was clean and bed was comfortable. AC and TV all worked well. The only downside was the shower drain was full of mold. I give 4.5 overall.

We were planning our trip to Byron Bay few months ago and we couldn’t found other dog friendly caravan park or accommodation in Byron Bay. But we were lucky to find Ingenia. We had very good experience in this place.

- Good facilities

Clean and shiny cooking and BBQ area with vending machine.
Clean toilets and bathrooms.
Basketball ground, swimming pool, kids playground and dog off leash area.
Coffee Cart.

- Convenient location.

Few minutes drive to town centre and other tourist destinations in Byron Bay.
About 10 minutes drive to A1 Pacific Highway.
10 minutes walk to Tallow Beach.

- Nice environment

Quiet, covered by lawn and trees.
Wild birds around.
Fresh air.

- Good customer services
Nice and friendly staff members.
They are willing to provide help.

By the way, our dog also want us to write this:
He made a lot of new friends in the caravan park and he wanna come back again with his humans. :-)
Hermoso camping. Buena zona para carpas, con pasto, árboles, naturaleza. Lo único malo son los baños. Quedaron chicos para tanta gente
Great dog friendly park. Location fantastic,  staff always friendly and accomdating. Clean facilities, so close to Byron Bay shops and beaches. Highly recommended

A very pleasant campsite with good showers and kitchens. We took the bus into Byron Bay for two days which works well. The staff are excellent, particularly the angel that handed in my son's watch that he left in the toilet.

Pathway to tallow beach is not accessible. Should have clarified that on the listing. The bathroom was moldy and had mold in ceilings and rubber edges. The kettle also had grime and slime within and we dare not use it. It was not the cheapest option but we had dog with us. The dog wash is actually a paid wash bay. Staff were friendly and welcoming though!

Hoewel het op papier allemaal mooi werd voorgesteld was voor ons de realiteit rampzalig. De uitrusting van de chalet was minabel, niet eens een tafel en stoelen binnen, kapotte linoleum, de keukenmeubels volledig opengespleten en vuile bakpan, enkel op de terras een vuile tafel en 3 stoelen. De badkamer vertoonde beschimmelde muren en beroeste handdoekdrager.De afstand naar Byron Bay is 4 km en korst bijgelegen  winkel 3km, dit is wat men noemt op wandel afstand.

Nice park, the self contained unit was quite adequate although the double bed was squashed in with no side tables. There was a fridge, microwave & elecric frypan which could be plugged in the outside deck power point. The distance to centre town was 3km and most parking was metered during daylight hours.
A downside to the park was access to the beach, the bridge was under repair, which meant a long walk to get there. I recommend the services club as a nice place to eat.

Lovely Little family holiday wss spent in a cabin . Close enough to the main town but far enough away to not be to noisy . Decent facility's , aircon was great ans and beds were clean and comfortable .we will be back !!
Expensive.  But dog friendly.  15 mins pushbike into town.  No beach access the bridge is down to get there
Bon camping mais un peu en dehors de la ville
personnel très gentil et agréable mais faite attention à vos affaires nous nous sommes fait voler notre glacière entière attention au cuisine commune ! ⚠️
Camping, a bit outside of the Byron bay but still easy to reach Byron bay by car, in about 10 minutes.
Der Camping ist sehr ruhig und sauber. Leider hat der Camping keinen eigenen Beach Acess. Aber mit dem Bus ist man in max. 10 min. In Byron Bay.

Leider viel zu wenig Duschen und Toiletten! Am Abend standen 5 Leute vor mir an und auch mein Bruder hat um 22:00 noch 15min auf eine freie Dusche gewartet. Es hat im unpowered Bereich nur lediglich 4 Duschen. Deutlich zu wenig. Das hatten wir in Australien bisher nirgends und damit leider eine schlechte Preis / Leistung - schade.

Personal freundlich und am Platz selbst gibt es nichts auszusetzen.

We really enjoyed our stay in the safari cabin and stayed for 2 nights for our anniversary. The cabin was clean and the park seemed very well kept. It was raining on arrival and rained alot through our stay so didn't get to explore the park and when it wasnt raining, we went into Byron Bay.
If you are a light sleeper,  ear plugs may help as cars driving past if you sleep early may keep you awake and depending on who's in the park, kids and louder adults may also disrupt your stay. Otherwise it was really quiet and quite private and we really enjoyed it 💕 would stay again for sure, there looked to be plenty of accommodation options that looked very cute!!!

Were here for 2 nights and super close to the town of Byron Bay tons of showers and washrooms spread out around the camp. Can't get to Tallow beach from here because the bridge has been eaten by termites. That beach is gorgeous!!

They have just grass sites which I think would be better as somesites seem to be grass and sand and your trailer can get very dirty! All depends how long you are here. We were fine.

Friendly staff and clean facilities.
Good camp site good facilities

It could be a nice campground with a lovely park, but the sites are really narrow and packed together, offering basically zero privacy.
There are only 10 showers for about 100 sites, and it’s impossible to have a shower in the afternoon: they’re always full and, when you finally get in, the water comes out in bursts. The shower is neither pleasant nor quick.
All the unpowered sites also have no access to running water, which is very inconvenient and makes the stay less comfortable.
The free Wi-Fi doesn’t work at all, which is quite disappointing.

Clean, recent 2 bedroom cabin with private parking, set in a quiet camping/holiday park. The cabin has a well-equipped kitchen and comfortable beds, good quality towels,
air con. Bathroom is adequate and water pressure pretty good for showering.  Wifi worked and we really enjoyed our stay. Friendly staff.

Nina on the desk was so lovely and helpful when we checked in, everything was explained clearly.

Very comfortable accommodation, just enough outside the town centre to avoid some of the chaos. Bedroom floors were a bit sandy on arrival, which i guess is expected in a beach town, but a bit unpleasant to walk in to.

Facilities are good, basic toiletries, tea and coffee provided. We did stay for 4 nights for a sports tournament and given the humidity, could have done with a few more towels but we were never at the accommodation while reception was open to request more, so thats not really on the accommodation, as im sure they would have supplied a couple extra if we had asked.

We had issues with across neighbours across the way from us parking their cars out front of their units, which made it really difficult to park our car in the carport, which was quite narrow, at times requiring quite a bit of back and forth and manoeuvring.

Lots of wildlife to be seen and heard, amd its lovely on the porch at night with the twinkle lights in the trees!

La zona de camping con power es terrible. En el medio de la tierra, lleno de lodo. Rodeado de bungalows, no sientes ambiente de camping. La “cocina” son las bbq que puedes encuentras en cualquier sitio de byron. Los baños bastante tercer mundistas. El staff es bastante grosero y te habla de malas formas. En especial un señor con bigote que nos amenazó varias veces, incluso con pegarnos. Creo que es el peor camping en el que he estado. Vete a discovery, mucho mejor!
Great campground 👏 Close beach walks, easy cycle into town with lots of dog activities.

Stayed in cabin 82 and was disappointed compared to every other Ingenia Caravan Park, staff lovely at check in ✅ the cabin was clean ✅ but the oven and stove top not working didn’t realise until after office had closed, no fry pan, 1 saucepan, 1 large lid for what we don’t know.  Couldn’t have used them any way, learnt check what’s working when you arrive. Had to go get takeaway not happy

We booked an ensuite powered site (because the regular powered sites were fully booked). We paid 104 dollars per night (!!) It was both the most expensive ánd the smallest spot we stayed at during our roadtrip. We were completely boxed in by huge caravans. Not very nice. The ensuite itself was very clean, but unfortunately the shower drain didn’t work properly, so you ended up standing in a pool of water. We didn’t see much of the campsite, as we spent most of our time in Byron Bay itself. (Byron is amazing)

We stayed here recently and the place is really, really good! The structure is amazing, great kitchen, laundry, pool, and plenty of spaces to sit, chat and relax. For families, there are so many options for kids: a small skate park, a little basketball court (or just space to play whatever they want), and a playground.

The toilets are a great size too, you can actually dry yourself properly without getting everything wet, which is a big plus! Everything is always super clean and tidy; the staff do an excellent job keeping the whole place well-maintained.

There are many accommodation options too: camping, glamping, rooms, houses, etc. The only downside for us was that the campsites are quite close to each other. You really feel like you’re sharing the same space, which takes away a bit of that “nature and peace” vibe we usually look for when camping. But it looks like there are some sites further away (around Jasmine Lane), so we’ll definitely try those next time!

For couples seeking the ultimate glamping experience, this is a must-visit.  Its great location offers a short drive to Byron Bay’s centre and serves as a perfect base for exploring nearby attractions.  A delightful surprise was the coffee cart caravan.
A little bit further from the beach but all good, the instalations were amazing, I'd definitely recommend this 👌🏻
Friendly service, site was fairly large and flat. Amenities were really good, spacious and clean

It's our 2nd time here, and we enjoyed our stay the same as last time  and are glad our furry babies can come along. There was a nespresso machine, and I took advantage of the pool. However, we wish we were on the quieter side. The whole time of our stay we could hear the lawn mower that woke us up. It was still good for the price. Would come back, but will request for a quieter location.

Very nice place for camping. They have clean and hot showers. Few camp kitchens. Make sure to book early enough as they close around 6 pm.
Beautiful place for walks and close to the beach too
Loved our stay here in a family villa. Much more spacious and well furnished than we expected. Friendly staff and peaceful.

We had a 6 night stay, booked a grassed site. Had I been told the grass was actually sand I would have booked a slab site. I didn't realise there was a superior area, I did booking over the phone no info given about different sites. The site has a mat to park on, it could do with another mat alongside to help cover the sand. It was very windy for two days and the sand blasted everything. Most sites are so close when you put the awning out your almost touching.
We were parked under a big tree full of birds. Yes the shade is nice but the bird poop not. Small branches and tree nuts and litter scattered the top of the van. Had we realised how noisy and messy it was we would have asked for a move. But once set up we couldn't be bothered moving, that's if they had a site available. I don't think they should put vans in this site 48. We are on the road for 12 months with limited opportunity to clean the van.
Amenities not sufficient for the amount of unpowered sites approximately 90. The toilets shower always disgusting with the campers just leaving facility unclean not bothering to use mop to clean floor after themselves. Needs more regular cleaning and more amenities.
There was another block by the glamping tents I preferred, it seemed much cleaner not used by the unpowered sites lots of backpackers there.
There was a pool, play ground and bouncy pillow that didn't seem to be inflated or used. They had a bridge access to the beach but this has been closed due to termite damage apparently.
Couldn't park car by van due to bird poop. After first night hubby took it to the car wash it was in such a mess. We left it in a parking bay by the reception.
I wouldn't stay here again, this has been the only park in over 5 months of travel I have been disappointed with.
Not sure why check in at 12 as sites vacated at 10 and not one person checked on or looked at the site from one leaving to another coming.
Best camp spot in Byron, organised, close to everything, 2 kitchens, pool, pet and family friendly

The toilet flush has almost no pressure so it just stops working at night time.
There are some lovely staff (the guys on the buggy carts and Rhi at the front desk) but every time we do visit we always remark on the attitude you seem to cop from the front desk. Always feels a little off putting as you’re there on holiday, paying a fair bit and you get treated like you’re an annoyance.
Anyway, we usually camp or stay in tallow pod both are amazing. This time I stayed in beach cabin 6. There were 4 centipedes (that I found, hate to think how many there really were) that I had to kill I saw one entering my room from what I assume was a hole behind the shelving. So gross. Apparently this hole was found and plugged up but there were still bugs so who knows. The cabin itself was so filthy, footprints all over the tiles when we arrived. It’s like no one really cares all that much about those cabins.
We do love it here because it has extremely special family memories and will be back, just will never book a cabin again, and hope and hope that we get looked after by Rhi again!

A very well-maintained and well-managed campground offering plenty of options for every type of traveler – from powered and unpowered sites to comfortable rooms and tent spaces. The facilities are clean and tidy, with toilets and showers that are regularly serviced, though a few more would be great during busy times. The campground has lots of nice extras such as a pizza van, bike rentals, and even skate sessions for kids, which makes the atmosphere lively and fun. The location is perfect – right by the beach, only about 10 minutes by bike to the city center, and a Woolworths just around the corner for all your supplies. The camp kitchen is spacious and well equipped, ideal for cooking together or meeting other travelers. Overall, a great place to stay with a relaxed vibe, friendly management, and everything you need for a comfortable beachside camping experience! Price was average but absolutely worth it.
Lovely showers and lounge spaces, as well as great kitchens with dish soap and fresh dish towels. Very quiet, nice walk out back and hopefully the bridge to the beach gets restored soon.

Very happy with our stay. We were on an unpowered site camping and had a great time. Toilet and showers were clean.

Would have been great to be able to access the beach but council as repairing bridge. Not the fault of the site though.

The staffs at reception were very rude and unrespectful. I could feel a bit of racism against immigrants. Very unpolite people. We've been camping years and years around Australia and all over the world and I never been treated like that before.
Lovely camping park. The most affordable in Byron too! Clean kitchens, toilets and general areas. I'd definitely recommend it! Also the sunsets look lovely!
Very nice campus, the room is okay
We were caravanning. Nice spot

A powered camp site (Ingenia Holidays Byron Bay) for $49 per night. They have clean toilet, showers and a dog wash area! Very pet friendly, very close to Tallow beach and Suffolk Park shops which is about 20-30min walk or a 5min drive from Ingenia Holidays park.

One of the best cabins we've stayed in this trip. We stayed 3 nights.||The caravan park is quite big and covers every type of holiday you are on or want from swags, tents, unpowered caravan sites, powered sites, sites with ensuites, basic cabins to large family cabins and glamping.||All the usual amenities, laundry, kitchens, pool, jumping mat, play ground.||Boom gated and only a few kms to town and a short walk to Tallow Beach but the bridge was closed while we were there.||Huge grounds, pet friendly and even had a dog agility area.||Our cabin was a family cabin, 1 x King Bed or large Queen in one room and 2 x Bunk Beds to sleep 4 and room 2.||Parents room had an ensuite with large walk in shower. Large wardrobe and plenty of storage. There was a second bathroom and toilet, laundry with washing machine and dryer. Complementary body wash, shampoo and conditioner.||The lounge and dining area had 2 chairs, a 3 seater couch, coffee table, dining table and 4 chairs.||Large kitchen, fridge, gas stove, oven, microwave, coffee machine and well as the usual instant and tea, sugar, salt and pepper.||Lots of cutlery, kitchen utensils etc.||Large smart TV.||Air-conditioning in main area as well as parents bedroom.||Large balcony with table and 4 chairs plus 2 loungers.||Car port for vehicle right beside cabin.||Friendly helpful  staff and reception. ||Thank you for a lovely stay, hopefully be back next year.

Spent 3 nights in a dog friendly cabin, with 12 yr,  2 teenagers and our dog during Sept school holidays.
Cabin was comfortable and well furnished.
Watched a brilliant reptile show put on for the school holidays.
A really pretty walk to the beach, however bridge was closed while we were there.  Lots of pretty trails to walk the dog.
Bus stop right outside the park for teens to get into town easily.
We had a lovely stay, caravan park is probably one of the best I've stayed at.
Yep good 👍

Nice little cabin with private parking, set in a quiet camping/holiday park. The unit is cozy and includes a well-equipped kitchen and a comfortable bed. On the downside, the bathroom is extremely small and the sink is so tiny that it’s hardly practical to use. Still okay for a short stay, but not ideal for longer visits.
Great spot  I’m a regular visitor and one of my favourite places
Great stopover, friendly staff!
Great caravan park if you are sleeping in your van, affordable and good amenities for a decent cost.
Very clean and nice place
Basic park, our caravan site flooded when it rained. Caters more for mobile homes on no power sites and cabins.

Thinking Luxury Camping?
This park has some amazing
'Safari Tent' options, that are really, very cool.💯
Beautiful accommodation within reach of everything Byron Bay has to offer.👌
4 Post Bed is delicious, truly ....I've tried a few !🤣
Fresh and clean bathroom with a deep, cast iron bath located on THE BALCONY.👍
Lay back in candlelight and enjoy your bath, all  whilst  listening to the waves crashing on the shore.✔️
BBQ, Microwave, kettle, t.v. full fridge, plus bar fridge and bbq/oven on the balcony.✔️
Well appointed, spotless and very comfortable.👌
You will enjoy💯
Very cute little park with well appointed apartments. Small but had everything we needed :)

Not recommended if it is raining, the sites are full of mud and sand, minimal grass. The access to the beach is closed so you need to drive 3kms to access the beach. Toilets and facilities were poorly maintained with the dump point overflowing.
Don’t let emergency service vehicles through the boom gate. Always a hassle to get a PIN number off them.
My fianee and I spent 2 nights and 3 days here. The beach access was closed so we had to drive 2.5km to visit the beach. Couldn't get netflix as there was no wifi. Cabin was cosy.

Park is very clean and well maintained and has a variety of accommodation, some pet friendly. Staff are courteous and helpful. Unfortunately the beach access is closed as the bridge is deemed unsafe. No timeframe as yet for rebuild. Also cafe pictured in website photos is no longer, but there are plans to reopen in the future.  The potato man was a fantastic feature and thoroughly enjoyed his loaded hot potatoes, the pizza van was ok, bit pricey $25.50 for what I got, vegetarian -onion, capsicum, mushrooms and cheese of course.
30 minute walk into Suffolk Park
Will be visiting again in the summertime with a view  to using the pool.

The park was ok.. It's not the parks fault its on a busy road.. we found byron Bay very, very busy. The park itself was alright, but we did hear construction workers, and this was even on a Sunday. The dump Point was overflowing to the point that people were using the toilets to empty their cassettes. When I first arrived, the lady behind reception was a little rude.. it felt like because we were a little early, i was an inconvenience. ( only on a powered site, no ensuite) Just didn't get a very nice vibe from her. When I went back, I got a younger lady who was much more approachable and very nice towards me. Amenities were clean and spacious.

We recently stayed at this caravan park for 3 nights. The staff were very friendly and helpful with information about local facilities like supermarkets etc. The sites are well maintained and there is an enclosed area for you to let your dogs have a run and play. Only disappointment was discovering that there is a $10 per night per dog fee which is not clear when making a booking as I only discovered it when I questioned the price discrepancy from what was advertised on the internet.

A group of us stayed this weekend, some in tents,some of us in small motorhomes. We could not fault the facilities, 4 small camp kitchens through out the park, 2 toilet/shower blocks near us. Great hot water and supplied body wash, shampoo and conditioner... amazing...never seen this before. Staff efficient and accommodating, only thing was the access to the beach, more than a 10 minute walk, lots of erosion and for us older chicks having to climb down a 3 metre drop and back up again.
Pet friendly would definitely come back again.

We paid for a premium powered caravan site which states on their website as being grassed, there is not one blade of grass on it , just a sand pit, for the prices they are charging, it is simply not good enough. It should be a slab site but if they are too mean for them at the very least they should be fully matted, and some do have some matting but it's too old and passed it's use by date, do not stay here
Perfect

Stayed two nights in July midweek still quite popular. Pleasant place still popular at this time of the year. Handy to everywhere in the region all the facilities you could need, we stayed in a cabin with our dog. This is a pet friendly place! Site has a pizzeria and bar
Lovely place in a very convenient location 👌

What a joke this place is, I reported a problem with birds (bin chickens) nesting in trees above the caravan parking area and pooing all over the caravan and cars parked near by, I was told a manager would contact me that day to probably move to a different spot, as they can't do anything about the birds in the locations, why the hell why wouldn't they tell people it is a problem with these bird as other caravan parks have done if thete is a problem with animals etc. It took a 5-year-old to sort out the problem to shift the birds from the trees of night time and the management didn't have the time to give me a call. Will never go back to that caravan park or recommend to my friends. I will send them the bill that it will cost me to get rid of all the shit over caravan and car.
Total awesome place to stay...very friendly and clean
Definitely recommend this place to all the parents who have kids more than one. There is plenty of playground swimming pool. And they do kids' activities as well during the school holiday.
Lovely place would stay there again
很不錯的體驗！
Nice cabins and grounds. Great service and pet friendly options.. Only down side is the traffic getting out the gate.. no traffic noise at all though..

We've stayed at several Ingenia Holiday Sites, and this is definitely one of my absolute favourites! Staff were accommodating and friendly. It's super tidy and clean, and the best part was how dog friendly it was! It's definitely one of my favorite holiday spots 🥰
Very friendly staff beautiful park thank you

The park itself seemed nice enough and the women at the front desk were lovely.

The ‘deluxe’ cabin we stayed in was far from deluxe. Severely overpriced for what we were given. Mould was EVERYWHERE in the cabin and the cabin was very run down.

Stains on sheets and cushion covers.

Would probably return to try the unpowered camp sites far down the back in our caravan but we will never stay in a cabin here again.
Had another fantastic stay at this place, booked the 2 room cabin this time and it felt like home. Very comfortable and clean, staff were lovely also.
Dryer not working so had to hang laundry and turned on AC. No lock on windows but overall great stay

+ Toiletten waren ausreichend vorhanden und wurden regelmäßig geputzt
+ Preis ist fair 40$ die Nacht
+ nah am Stand
+ großzügige Cabins

- Internet sehr schlecht oft nicht verfügbar
Our pod was awesome 👌

We stayed at Ingenia Holiday Park in Byron Bay and honestly couldn’t be happier. It was super affordable and had everything we needed: clean facilities, all the amenities really well looked after, and a really chill vibe. Everyone we spoke to, from staff to other guests, was super friendly and helpful. The whole place felt very relaxed and welcoming. The location is also great, close to town but quiet enough to rest properly. We were really impressed and would definitely stay here again!

What a wonderful caravan site. Receptionist were very helpful, park super clean and tidy, massive pitches, very quiet and a short walk to the beach. We needed to stay another night and staff were very quick and helpful. I would recommend Ingenia Bryon Bay.
Thank you for a wonderful time 😊

I had a great stay at ingenia. But I accidentally left my book there and tried to reach them via online inquiry form twice and also left a voicemail since no one answered the call, after three attempts and no replies at all, i am sorry to leave a bad experience about their services.

For low budget camping, it's the ideal place. The grounds are equipped with everything needed. It has several camp kitchens. It's quiet, the international travellers are quiet. A lot of tourists are in Byron Bay and staying at the holiday park. The staff are very friendly and helpful. Overall, we enjoyed our 4 nights stay and we thank you for accommodating us.

Couldn’t be happier with our stay in the glamping tents. They have absolutely thought of everything. Kitchen exclusive for the glamping tents fully stocked with plates/utensils. They also have pop up food trucks arrive eg pizza truck for dinner or coffee cart for breaky. This is our new yearly girls trip. Couldn’t be happier.

Caravan sites seem to flood easily due to lack of maintenance..  sites have big dips in them that could be resolved with more top soil or blue metal. When it rains water pools around the van. In our case our 15m electrical lead was left floating. When I complained I was told there wasn’t anything they could do. Was also told water soaks away quickly… if 6 to 8 hours is quick, fair enough. All of our mats etc were filthy and full of muddy sand, as was inside the van. Virtually no grass to be seen on what was a grass site. Young guy on side by side seemed more interested in his mate in  a caravan than looking after customers. Pool freezing with not 1 person in it. Park is a long way from Byron as well… beach hotel is about an hours walk from the park at 4,5km away… not sure where they get 3 km away from.  I’d avoid this place and stay at a park closer in.
The swimming pool was a bonus. Bus was easy to catch into town. Good facilities and nice walk to the beach in the evening!
Great spot out of the busyness of the centre of Byron. Nice walk/run into town and some good coffee just down the road.

We hadn't originally planned to come to this campsite but we walked past and asked for the price, as it was the cheapest rate we received so we stayed. We paid $40 a night for a place to stay without electricity. We were already very happy to pay less than the other campsites. Then there were a lot of semi-covered camp kitchens which was really good, with a few barbecues. For the showers/toilets it is a shared place for men and women but in fact these are lots of small shower rooms with sink and toilet, I found that really good. Finally the staff were friendly. You should know that there is a fairly busy road along the campsite but it is very quiet at night. I think it is good value for money

Great park, extremely clean and well appointed with excellent facilities.
Dog friendly.

What a great spot! 5 mins out of town with super clean and new facilities, with easy access and parking. Especially when you get help reversing your caravan into its spot. I'll give that help an extra 5 stars as I didn't hit anything or have an argument with my wife 🤣

Not too bad, superior cabin was very basic and needed a bit of maintenance, however was relatively comfortable and met our needs. We were allocated probably the only cabin without a reasonable parking space out the front, which was a little bit frustrating when other people were not considerate with their parking.

We zijn 1 nacht geweest op een powered site met een camper. Het was een mooie camping waar je zeker met kinderen leuk kunt vertoeven. De douches waren schoon en er werd shampoo/ zeep aangeboden! De campkitchen was ook erg schoon en genoeg ruimte.
De ligging is prima naar het centrum van Byron bay als je een auto hebt.

We were unfortunate enough to book a long weekend that the Glamping Tents were booked out by a very noisy, uncourtious bunch of people ( who played loud music to all hours into the am, with no apprehension from the park staff. 3 nights straight!!

I stayed at the campground with my friends and I was so impressed by the cleanliness of all the amenities and facilities! Good hot showers as well and you get to have a great wifi connection!

Also, the bus stop is right in front of the resort, so everything is convenient over there!

So if ever you stop by Byron Bay, I would recommend this place 100%

First the good things:

Very dog friendly with a agility park in the back which we loved!!
They Note down the breed and the shape etc for if we lose our dog to help us looking (thankfully not needed but a very nice gesture)
Couple minute walk from the beach (off leash) which is incredible
Reception was very friendly
Park looks very nice and some trees have lights around them

Not so good things:

Our site was very small (103) hard to back into and we drive a 19 foot caravan and had problems fitting our car on the site
The amenities where quiet dirty on both of our nights stay and there’s mold in the showers

Over all we enjoyed our stay!
Great holiday park for the family
Huge variety of different accommodation options.. friendly staff.. not too car from Byron central..
Had everything you needed for a campsite

Came to Byron Bay for Bluesfest. Stayed with 4 other families, great sites for caravans and a
Camper trailer. Love being able to do some washing in the laundry - and the cannot gen was great too.
Probably the worst CV park I have stayed at . It is just like free camping but not free or clean or any good ! It has a mineral pool or at least I think that’s what is in it . Absolute grub .
The cleanest dog friendly cabin in Byron . Beautifully located near beach with all amenities and dog wash faculties . Well priced . Ingenia we need more of you
You have to pay for parking in Byron bay
Camping langs een drukke weg. Faciliteiten zijn niet geweldig. Gevoel dat je in een soort hostel zit, helaas geen aanrader vanuit onze kant

Stayed 1 night unpowered site with our 3 dogs. Park is very clean and staff very helpful. Dog exercise yard for the pups to run around off lead and a short walk to a beautiful beach for dogs as well. Lots of different accomodation to suit anyone and have loved more time to stay. Highly  recommend this park for a holiday.

Thank you for an amazing stay! The lady’s on front desk couldn’t be any more nicer if they tried, truly beautiful people! Accomodation was perfect and I wish I was still there but I’ll definitely be coming back! Thank you! Marty

Stayed here when we were in Byron. Facilities were all good, enjoyed our stay and felt safe with the barrier. A bit far from the town but there is a bus you can get that stops right outside but only runs until 5pm. Also has a nice pool!

Ein wirklich toller und netter Campingplatz, leider waren die Duschen und Toiletten nicht sehr sauber. Eine Bushaltestelle ist direkt an der Straße und man kommt super mit den öffentlichen Verkehrsmitteln nach Byron Bay. Zum Strand läuft man 15 Minuten. Man kann auch Fahrräder ausleihen, was für uns aber pro Tag für 45$ zu viel war. Man bekommt eine Karte mit vielen Aktivitäten in der Nähe, das ist wirklich super und interessant.
A dream holiday home. From the service of the lovely people, the amazing area, to the breathtaking houses/cabins. It is all just perfect. We even got an upgrade! Highly recommend it ✨

We were very impressed with how tidy and clean the villa and surrounds were, it was truly a pleasure to stay here.
The manageress could not have been more helpful if she thought something had gone wrong.
We shall be back and would recommend it to anybody who plans on staying in Byron Bay
Michael and Amanda Price

Amazing Park lots of greenery.
Clean Amenities great staff helpful and sweet always cleaning.
Awesome pool, Our caravan spot 211 was amazing.
Short walk to beach
would definitely come back and would def recommend.
ONLY CARAVAN PARK IN BYRON BAY ALLOWS PETS ONSITE

I will never come back to this place. The only word I have to describe the staff is racist. We stayed in a cabin, and a friend came to visit us but accidentally entered another one. What followed was three staff members yelling at us and accusing us of trying to scam them by sneaking in people who hadn’t paid for their stay, even though our friend was just visiting.

Two days later, when we were checking out, a blonde woman with glasses followed us while we were just trying to have breakfast in the common area. She told us that if we didn’t leave, she would call the police, claiming that during the previous misunderstanding, we had thrown a chair at a manager. This is a lie. She also accused us of being six people in one cabin, which is also false since we were two groups of friends staying in two separate cabins and would simply gather to eat and hang out.

We realized that the disrespect wasn’t just toward us but also toward other foreign guests. For example, there was a couple who just wanted to charge their phone, but they weren’t allowed to because they had already checked out, and they were told that if they didn’t leave, the police would be called.

It’s shameful to treat guests this way for no reason. It is unacceptable. Bunch of racists and liars exaggerating everything to kick us out.

Good location if you have a car or a bicycle.
Clean rooms, great facilities, and nice walk to the beach via the lagoons.
Downside is it can be hard to get a park if you have two cars.

This is a magical place I look forward to going back to time and time again. I have been going here for years and have watch it develop while staying true to the original character of the park. The walk through the wetlands to the beach is the highlight as is the welcoming attitude to dogs including a secure dog area. Some rooms are more affordable and older in style but still clean and neat as a pin and mostly importantly, more affordable so I do hope they keep the older rooms. PS all the beds are new and insanity comfy!! The most peaceful and quiet of parks where you can drift away and only hear the wind in the trees. Highly recommend!!

3 nachten geslapen, ruime plaatsen. Schone voorzieningen en goede douche. De mensen zijn vriendelijk.  De prijs is goed vergeleken met andere campings in de buurt, scheelde 80 dollar per nacht!

Ze hebben een zwembad, speeltuin, basketbalveld en een klein skatepark. Absoluut een aanrader dus.
Great place for staying.  Short walk to beach.  Short drive to town. Easy check in . Thanks

Our èxcuse was my 60th birthday.  We stayed in Tallow Pod. This unique 'cabin' is without doubt the most beautiful  'cabin' style accommodation that we've  had the pleasure  of staying in.
Modern, fresh and clinically clean, this accommodation  was so good that it demanded we spend more time at 'The Pod', than we had originally planned. Spacious  with well appointed  bathroom and full kitchen including dishwasher. Close to the beach (10 min stroll down the track) and we could hear the soothing sound of the ocean for the entirety of our 4 night stay.

It was very hard to leave this place and you will love it. Particularly Romantic, as it is designed to accomodate  a couple and huge living and bedroom area make this Pod the most ideal place for a special  celebration for 2.
Unfaultable and grand, whilst submerged in nature, this pet friendly park is well organised and operated, with any request attended to, with ease.
Ideally situated a 3 min drive from the hustle of Byron Bay Central, this park is peaceful, so much so that You can hear the surrounding nature. Highly recommended

Booked a powered site 10 months ago, to have it re-booked at higher/new price, as they moved us to a new site. Questioned this on the phone and original price honoured. Were told on arrival that we were moved to a "premium site" that goes for $140.00 per night, but we would get for weekend rate of $55.00. The site had no slab as it is a "national park", but we can have a dog! It was sandy and stoney and extremely small. The amenities were very dated. Needed to request replacement of toilet paper over 2 days. The staff are very busy doing not very much. Blow vac leaves in the same area for 10 mins, then to a new area for 10 min each day. So not relaxing at all!
Staff in golf buggies yelling at other staff members in golf buggies, appearing to take the Mickey out of the  job. Staff taking awhile to clean the toilets but faecal matter still on the bowl when they leave.

Very disappointing experience. Will not be back
Lidt slidt

We camped here in a powered site for 2 nights and we were really impressed. The facilities were clean and staff were so helpful. Unfortunately the nearby beach and main beaches in Byron had an influx of blue bottle jellyfish so we asked the front reception if there were any other options and they gave us a list of alternatives. The only complaints I had:
- closest beach (which is stunning) is a 10 min walk but the trail to the beach has various warnings about brown snakes in the area and we were not warned.
- we got lots of ibis’ nesting in the trees at night above our campsite - lots of bird poo and noise (but really - you cant help nature)

I would definitely recommend. There are cooking facilities, a pool, showers and toilets that are cleaned daily.

Customer service is very good, Stayed in a demountable cabin on the way through on a trip. Air can was fantastic the bed terrific, Water pressure was crap and the shower toilet set up laughable. How close can you get the toilet bowl to the wall???? Well, they did it, and sitting on the wall was an adventure using said toilet. Park is 5 minutes by car from the centre of town but couldn't wait to leave. The park is tired and worn out.

Die Lage ist ok. Der Strand ist zu Fuss zu erreichen was toll ist (ca 10 Minuten). Die Stadt sowie der Leuchtturm ebenfalls auf einem Fussweg erreichbar aber es ist schon etwas weit weg. Der Camping an sich ist gut, die Stromsäulen sind aber weit von den Parzellen weg und die WC- Anlagen sind eine Katastrophe - alt, kaputt und die Wasserhähne funktionieren nur wenn man sie hält. Eher schwierig zum Händewaschen 🙈
Eigentlich ein guter Campingplatz. Die sanitären Anlagen waren aber das Schlimmste was wir in 5 Wochen Australien gesehen haben. Völlig runtergekommen und keine Reinigung erkennbar.
Eigentlich ein schöner Ort. Leider sind die Sanitäranlagen wirklich total heruntergekommen.

Had great experience staying with kids in one of the cabins. The ocean is walkable distance or 5 min drive around. There is an off-leash beach for your dog as well.

The site has got a pool, an inflatable jumping pad, a playground and a small skate park - enough to keep kids entertained. The area is quite clean overall.
Bon emplacement à 10 mn a pieds de la plage.

Not enough amenities for the amount of campers. The amenities closest to the camp grounds had wooden floors which stunk of urine. We were lucky to find a cleaner shower on the other side of the park however there was still signs of mold everywhere. The sites are pretty small with no wiggle room. Had campers let in after 9pm who set up their tents until 11pm. Was very disappointed with our stay.
Not far from a beautiful beach but along the busy road. Noisy. Otherwise ok.

We stayed in one of the newer duplexes which are nice, we made good use of the deck with lockable bar fridge and BBQ.

They have food trailers arrive throughout the week providing a variation of foods and desserts.

Wifi worked on the deck outside but not inside the duplexes.

If you have any issues just see the staff our shower drain was blocked when we arrived but they had maintenance action it quickly.

Fantastic park! We brought our caravan for a 5 night stay and the sites were reasonable size and the location is beautiful. Peaceful area and beach walk lovely. Amenities were great being community ensuite bathrooms which is great when you have young kids. The pool is lovely to cool off in. The staff were lovely and helpful and attentive especially in the epic Strom we were here in. Young guys with the dog was very helpful (sorry didn't catch his name). Will definitely come back for more visits. Value for money was exceptional. Thank you team.

Stayed in a duplex pod, which had a modern feel. Shower did overflow on the first night as the drain must have been blocked. Maintenance fixed it the next day so that was all good. No daily housekeeping so bits of sand, dust etc. would eventually build up. Would stay here again.

If you value your personal safety and property don’t book a premium caravan site.flocks of birds get up in the abundant amount of large pine trees and drop pine cones like bombs on your caravan and car, even on you if you stand there!!
When I raised the issue with the office staff I was told it’s not their problem. Apparently it’s national park in that area. Which was a lie after calling the local council.
I mentioned to the office staff that at a minimum people should be warned when booking sites…she laughed and replied if we did that nobody would come!!!..I went back to Melbourne with a dented car and van.
Byron bay is absolutely beautiful, but stay away from ingenia..plenty of other parks in the area
Cheers Andrew
The best of the best. Highly recommend. So peaceful,clean, and energetic place.

I’d avoid this caravan park and stay at others in Byron bay. The entire park is full of trees with sharp pine cones that fall all over the cars and caravans. We had a group of 4 families and paid extra to stay on their “premium sites” which resulted in all our cars being dinted from the branches and pine cones that constantly drop from the trees. Our friends caravan awning has a massive hole from the cones also. My husband went up to reception wanting to speak with the manager but of course the manager wasn’t available. We then advised it would have been nice if we were pre warned about the amount of debris and pine cones the trees drop, and I quote, the receptionist said to my husband “if we warned everyone about the trees, we wouldn’t have any customers” we will never stay here again and will be taking this matter further. The caravan park won’t take responsibility however, a caravan park is private property which means, it’s absolutely their responsibility. If you have small children and pets avoid this park. It’s not worth having a spikey pine cone drop on your child or pets head and causing serious damage. Old and dated facilities, out of order laundry facilities and the dump point was overflowing so we couldn’t empty our toilet.

Beach shacks:

Stayed 4 nights in a Beach Shack.

Room appeared tidy, but floors weren't well cleaned. A lot of sand felt on the floor as soon as we walked in. The bed also had sand in it.

1 pillow case had a questionable blood looking smear on it. 1 towel had a questionable stain on it. Went to the shops to buy new bedding and towels so we didn't have to use those provided with the accomodation.

The kitchen and bathroom ceilings had visible signs of mold.

The bathroom was less than 1m wide by less than 2m. It contained a toilet, sink and shower. There was nowhere to store any toiletries and was super confined in size.

There was nowhere to store clothes/luggage.

There were no conveniently located power points. Including none in the bathroom that I could see, and none beside the bed. Had to use the reflection in the microwave door to get ready as there was at least 1 power point there I could use.

The bed was on wheels, and on a hard floor and moved all over the room while we slept. Super annoying and uncomfortable when sleeping.

No where in the room to sit except for the bed because had to use 'couch' and dining chairs to store luggage.

Nowhere to hang wet beach towels/wet clothes outside the room.

For the price, the room was terrible and only in an okay location. I've backpacked overseas and stayed in far better hostels that have been cheaper and more centrally located.

Wouldn't stay at the accomodation again and can't recommend it to anybody. I would stay clear of Ingenia Holiday Parks.

Ein großer Platz mit einigen Attraktionen für Kinder. Der Amenities Block bei den Unpowered Sites ist Unisex und war mittelmäßig sauber. Man läuft etwa 10 min zum Strand und hat einen guten Blick auf den Leuchtturm von Byron Bay.

The glamping tents were outstanding. There was parking on site and shared bathroom facilities. The bathrooms were quite clean but very busy. The tent was clean and upscale, and felt very comfortable. It did rain the first night, which did wake me up, but otherwise, I had an excellent experience here. The location was about a 10 minute walk to the beach, or a 10 minute drive to the city centre.

This was our 6th stay here, and the most disappointing. The cabin size is in no way suited for 4 ppl. Cabin itself is outdated, uncomfortable and extremely overpriced.
First time that I was looking forward to going home.

Good location with walking access to the beach and parking available. We stayed for a few nights in a cabin, and the accommodation initially appeared clean. However, we found several small cockroaches in the bathroom at night, which significantly impacted our experience.

The camp in general was too dusty, wich made everything and everyone dirty.|Toilets in our area were bad.|At least staff was really friendly.|This is particularly relevant since we were charged 249 AUD/night, we have been in a lor of camps that for half the price were much better.

Caravan Park overall was ok, pretty good location, however site was tiny, only just fitting caravan and awning and no room for car. Awning area was soft uneven and dusty.
Park was one of most expensive i have ever paid, and doesn't live up to that!

Camping of mediocre quality. The sanitary facilities are dirty, smell bad, and are insufficient in number. The pitches are small, and most are not shaded. The path is not paved, and when vehicles pass by, there is dust everywhere. The camp kitchens are small, outdated, and dirty.
The game room is tiny and in a deplorable state.

I wouldn't stay again. Probably the worse campsite we've stayed in on the east coast. Expensive unpowered site backed onto a main road, so there was constant loud noise ~ barely slept and have a thumping headache. Sites are so cramped you can hear the neighbouring campers breathing. People up all night, woo-hooing, slamming van doors and lurking around our site. The toilet and shower blocks are filthy, with rubbish, mould, stained walls; we even spotted a smear of shit. Underwhelmed and disappointed. There are no cut-through paths to the toilet, making it a long trek on gravel roads in the middle of the night if you didn't want to inconvenience others.

Premium caravan sites not so Premium!
Staff at reception lovely however this park is in big need of some TLC. We have stayed in many caravan parks and this has to be the worst of the bunch! The dirty dusty site they put us on was disgraceful for the price we paid.... not to mention the cockys in the trees dropping fresh pine cones all over our caravan roof at 6am. Facilities are in need of a revamp... the playground, the pool and toilets all very average

Noisy even past 10pm at campsite and couldn't find security around. Bunch of boys howling and making barking noises at 11pm at camp kitchen when trying to find security. Amenities not the best compared to other Ingenia properties. Beach is quite some distance away and there are boards to watch out for brown snakes along the way to the beach.

It was ok. The amenities are old and not especially clean. Camp kitchens were gross, bins were stinky, sites were ok. Sadly guests were unfriendly and all up not the best getaway. Byron in general disappointed tbh, impossible to get a park, paid ridiculous prices for 5 and 10 minute stops. Park out of town so not driving was not an option. All up a let down.
Extremely overpriced for what it is. Pictures are very deceiving. stayed in a small sandy site that was extremely tight. Amenities were extremely dirty.
Good experience. A little bit overpriced

Clean and comfortable place to stay very close to Byron bay. There is a bus stop right outside but the buses are not very frequent. We were allowed to leave our bags at the reception for free after checkout which was useful.The beach is a short walk away (though do be careful of snakes. I’d recommend taking the pathed route) which was great for seeing sunrise.
Absolutely love this place! Friendly and helpful staff, great amenities and just far enough out of town for us. Can't wait to come back!

Great Holiday park stayed here to explore Byron bay & the park did not disappoint being a short drive/ride to see all there is to see do in Byron bay and surrounding areas. Park is so pretty at night with fairy lights

Nos echaron de allí por ser extranjeros. Les dijimos que nos pasábamos el día trabajando y que solo íbamos a dormir, y nos respondieron que les daba igual, que no querían mochileros por allí. Sentimos el racismo en sus palabras.

We stay there for vacations. The instalations are ok (dirty jitchen and dirty bathrooms) but the wors part are tha manager and nogth manager. Obly because they listened us speaking spanish they discriminate us.

Staff is racist and rude, specially for the manager and the “night manager”. They started looking for excuses to kick us out and they try to make sure people from different parcels not to get along. We used the kitchen on a Saturday night and they said we couldn’t. Everything with rudeness. I’m sure they wouldn’t do any of this if we were elder people instead. And they've done the same to other campers this past week. We’re never coming back to this place.
Nous avons passé 2 nuits en van , le personnel est super , notre emplacement était bien , il y a 3 spot pour cuisiner.

Oh I do hate leaving bad reviews :-( 220 for a room that allows a pet. I felt like I was in a box. The room had no love inside, very basic and boxy.
Some bottled water would be nice in the fridge, for this price.
Bit of a longer trek to the beach though gorgeous once there. Thanks.

Must say my experience here was very disappointing. All showers and toilets are mixed. Both my 11 year old daughter and myself felt very uncomfortable having to shower right next door to the old men that were there. The shower block just stunk of marijuana every time I went in. We also felt very dirty as there is no paths. After getting out of the shower we had to walk back through dirt and sand to get back to our site. Lastly when we booked in I told them my father couldn't walk and was in a chair. They still told us we could get to the beach very easy. That was a lie! You had to walk through scrub for around 10 mins before getting near the beach. When your finally there don't forget the heap of stairs you have to climb. My father could get no where near the path let alone the beach. Overall I was disappointed all around. Won't be returning here.

Wir waren mit einem Motorhome 2 Nächte dort und hatten eine Site mit eigener Toilette und Dusche. Diese waren zwar sauber, aber in die Jahre gekommen. Unser Stellplatz selbst machte wie der Untergrund in der Umgebung einen eher ungepflegten Eindruck. Die am Stellplatz ausgelegten grünen Matten erfüllten ihren Zweck nicht, waren mit grossen Löchern versehen und eher Stolperfallen.
Für uns der schlechteste Ingenia auf unserer seitherigen 5-wöchigen Tour ab Melbourne!
Excellent service, clean rooms, close to the beach, loads of nature and dog friendly. Love love love

Absolutely loved our stay here! We had a little hiccup with the hot water in our cabin, but Zane and the team really went above and beyond to make things right. They quickly moved us to another cabin, all while being super understanding and genuinely apologetic. You can tell a lot about a place by how they handle issues, and this crew really showed they care. Great location, amazing service – highly recommend this campground and more importantly the people who run it. Thanks Zane and the rest of the team.
Lovely place, would definitely go back

We stayed on a powered site with our toddler and dog and overall had a nice stay. Staff were lovely and happy to help with a couple of queries, there were decent amenities provided and the park had a nice vibe. |Our only issues were the sand on our site (a mat or concrete slab would have been less messy) and the walk to the beach - although ‘beach access’ is advertised, it’s still a 20 min walk from the park.
All in one toilet/shower rooms, very clean, nice spots

Squeezed into small site just enough room to have awning ropes in the ground, had to put car in front of the caravan half in the road. Unisex amenities across the whole park cleaned daily but visible mould in each cubicle should spend more time cleaning them. Sites need mowing when someone leaves only witnessed this once over the week we were there overall good but could be a lot better.

Just outside town. Quiet, pool, jumping pad for the kids, 10 minute walk to the beach, Amenities were cleaned daily.
Service was Wonderful. There is a coffee cart every morning. A 5 minute drive into Byron.
We will stay again next time we travel.

Not recommended.

Glass strewn all through the site. We continued to find more each day.
My partner informed them about this, and they said that they would rake the site to remove it, but never did, despite having 5 days to do so.

Mould in the fridges and the bathroom mould is straight out of 'the last of us'

Good facilities, though they have been run down over the past couple of years.

Was there for 1 night (less than 16 hours in total). KIDS (pre teens) AROUND THE PARK WERE RUDE, acting like they run the place, and parents were abnoxious. Was an absolutely horrible encounter around the pool.

To add, PICTURES DO NOT MATCH THE ROOM. The toilet was on the wall and the floor was dirty.
Would have been ok, if they had been more HONEST with their advertising of rooms.

'superior' was not superior at all, it seems this only means the addition of a single bed.

Couldn't leave early enough. Visitors to the country, we were appauled, not sure how the room with that bathroom gets complicance.
Clean cabbens, well kept facilities, all round grate expirence

My partner and I stayed in the beach shack for 5 days & 4 nights.
The location and staff were friendly.
Horrible toilet facility as you can see in the photo it was like 2 inches from the wall and you couldn’t sit in the toilet correctly
There was mold on the bathroom roof and kitchen walls
The shower curtain was a pain in the ass because it was way to close
The mattress was the best I had great night sleeps & the pillows were amazing.

I would recommend stay at this place and I would like to go back but I would get a upgrade from the beach shack as it was way to small for my partner and I

Caravan park offering beautiful walk through bird life and natural habitat straight through to the beach.
We stayed for two nights in the standard cabin. It was average but clean and tidy. Great location only a 15 min drive to the main street. What a wonderful place to go for a weekend.👍

They advertise they have a cafe which has been closed for months, dog wash broken and jumping castle is not in operation. The spot light on the basketball/skate park so kids can play all night long is not great for the ambience and loud noise all hrs into the night! Also where is the parents to their under 12 yr olds being left unattended in the pool? This park is a hike from town and the amenities are dated looks like they are just concerned for the cabins here at this park, they really need to update their facilities.

We stayed in a superior family cabin, and the place was exceptionally clean and tidy. Check-in was very easy. While the rooms are small, they are spotless, with modern fittings and great lighting.

We stayed for two nights, and although it rained heavily both days, we had to spend most of our time in the cabin. Thankfully, the cabin was so comfortable that it made the disappointment of the weather much easier to handle. Each cabin has its own parking spot, and they even provide EV chargers for each carport. The location is close to town, but far enough to avoid the busy atmosphere.

Overall, it’s a lovely place for a family stay. Highly recommend it. I wish I had taken pictures, but the ones on the website provide an accurate representation of the cabin.

We had a very rustic uneven caravan site and toilets though clean were in desperate need of an upgrade. Disappointed that cafe advertised had shut down too.
Overall very pet friendly park, staff at reception helpful and friendly and a nice beach to walk too. But we felt that too much money has gone into cabins and more funds were needed for upgrading camping and caravan sites and toilets.

Last time I was here was in 2001 and had stayed many times before that..

Never has it been so efficiently and cleanly run, staff are amazing and super friendly and amenities all highly maintained..

Great for kids and families too..

We stayed with our dog in an umpowered site and will be booking in again any time we are in Byron..

Great family friendly park. Our villa was very clean, great lay out, could comfortably stay a week. The beach is a bit of a walk down a back track but not too bad. Bit far too walk to town but quick drive. Coffee shops in walking distance. Only down side for us was noisy neighbours not respecting the rules coming back early hours of morning being rowdy, and leaving front lights on ALL night. Great pool area with lounges and grassed sections. Would definitely recommend and stay again. Priced well for byron!

Nice and clean|Everything you need for a get away|I was in cabin and very comfortable.  I did want glamping| tent but glad as when looked were mouldy|Close to town and beach|Nice pizza place on site|Didn’t like how they kept sending two or three texts everyday trying to push sale for massage, pizzas etc

Amazing place, but what truly sets this place apart are the staff. I could name names but isn’t it better you come here & find out for yourselves. If there were more stars to give I would , these guys deserve it.
Older cabin. Could do with a bit of maintenance. Staff were great.
Unisex showers and toilet combined facilities. Large plots. Close to a nice beach. Good for families and couples. Nice amenities. Price is fair
Quiet location great for families

We stayed on a powered site with our caravan for 6 nights and loved it. Spot was a little tight as there were trees in front and sites are side by side so was hard to back in with other peoples vehicles etc, but we made it.

Staff were really lovely and friendly, the campsite itself is really nice and the location was only a 6 min drive out of town which is easy when you've got a car or a bike to ride in on. With the 'stay 4 nights and get 20% off' deal it was really cost-effective too.
